content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Monopole Magnets". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1345/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains two integers n and m. The next n lines contain a string each, representing the rows of the grid.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of moves required to make all the monopole magnets have the same polarity.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use BFS to find the shortest path between each pair of '*' in the grid, then use the Floyd-Warshall algorithm to find the minimum number of moves required to make all the monopole magnets have the same polarity.
